title: goodenia heterophylla subsp. montana
intro: Goodenia heterophylla subsp. montana, also known as the mountain goodenia, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Goodeniaceae. It is native to Australia and has been used in traditional medicine for centuries due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The mountain goodenia has been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, and is believed to have antioxidant and immune-boosting properties. In herbal preparations, the mountain goodenia is often made into a tea or infused in oil to take advantage of its medicinal properties, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety.
